metadata.summary: Bootstrap pytest for a Python project: plugin selection, pyproject.toml configuration, project structure, coverage gates, parallel execution with xdist, CI integration, and agentic TDD workflow.

content.title: pytest Project Setup тАФ Plugins, Configuration, Coverage, and CI
content.summary: Wire pytest into a Python project: install the essential plugin set, configure pyproject.toml with strict markers and coverage gates, structure the tests/ directory mirroring src/, add parallel execution for CI, and follow the agentic TDD loop for feature development.

why.p: pytest out of the box works, but a properly configured project prevents whole classes of maintenance problems: strict-markers catches typos in marker names at collection time, filterwarnings = ["error"] turns deprecation warnings into failures before upgrades break you, branch coverage detects untested conditionals, and pytest-randomly exposes order dependencies before they become production flakes. The plugin ecosystem in 2025-2026 has matured to the point where each concern has a focused, well-maintained plugin.

list.item: New Python project тАФ wire pytest as the only test runner; never use stdlib unittest for new code.
Migrating from unittest.TestCase тАФ keep existing tests running, add new tests in pytest style (the mix is supported).
CI pipeline that needs coverage reporting, parallel execution, or artifact upload.
Team project that needs enforced marker discipline and reproducible test ordering.
Doctest-only libraries тАФ pytest can run doctests but adds setup overhead; pure "python -m doctest" is sufficient for tiny libs.
Single-script tools where the setup overhead dominates тАФ assert + manual run is fine.
BDD-style requirement docs тАФ pytest-bdd works but behave is more idiomatic for Gherkin-first teams.
Serious performance benchmarks тАФ pytest-benchmark works but asv (airspeed velocity) or vendor profilers give better regression detection.
Hard-real-time or hardware-in-loop tests тАФ pytest's discovery and fixture model add latency; use a dedicated harness.

section.name: essential-plugins
section.p: Essential plugin set (2025-2026) with monthly download counts as a popularity signal:

list.item: pytest-cov: coverage reporting, 87M+ downloads/month.
pytest-xdist: parallel execution via -n auto, 60M+.
pytest-asyncio: async test support, 58M+.
pytest-mock: mocker fixture wrapping unittest.mock, 50M+.
pytest-django: Django integration (db, transactional_db, settings fixtures), 20M+.
pytest-timeout: prevent hanging tests, 19M+.
pytest-randomly: randomize test order to expose order dependencies, 10M+.
pytest-sugar: improved output formatting, 8M+.
pytest-factoryboy: auto-register Factory Boy factories as pytest fixtures, 5M+.

commands.command: uv add --dev pytest pytest-cov pytest-asyncio pytest-mock pytest-randomly pytest-xdist
uv add --dev pytest-timeout pytest-sugar syrupy hypothesis

section.name: pyproject-config
section.p: Complete pyproject.toml configuration covering test discovery, output, markers, async, warnings, coverage, and reporting:

example.title: pyproject.toml тАФ complete pytest and coverage configuration
example.code:
[tool.pytest.ini_options] # Test discovery testpaths = ["tests"] python_files = ["test_*.py", "*_test.py"] python_classes = ["Test*"] python_functions = ["test_*"]
# Output addopts = [ "-v", "--tb=short", "--strict-markers", "--strict-config", "-ra", # Show summary of all except passed ]
# Markers (--strict-markers enforces this list) markers = [ "slow: marks tests as slow (deselect with '-m \"not slow\"')", "integration: marks tests as integration tests", "unit: marks tests as unit tests", "e2e: marks tests as end-to-end tests", ]
# Async asyncio_mode = "auto" asyncio_default_fixture_loop_scope = "function"
# Warnings тАФ turn deprecations into failures early filterwarnings = [ "error", "ignore::DeprecationWarning:pytest_asyncio.*", "ignore::PendingDeprecationWarning", ]
minversion = "8.0" timeout = 300 # 5 minutes default (requires pytest-timeout)
[tool.coverage.run] source = ["src"] branch = true parallel = true omit = [ "*/migrations/*", "*/tests/*", "*/__init__.py", "*/conftest.py", ]
[tool.coverage.report] exclude_lines = [ "pragma: no cover", "def __repr__", "if self.debug:", "raise AssertionError", "raise NotImplementedError", "if TYPE_CHECKING:", "class .*\\bProtocol\\):", "@(abc\\.)?abstractmethod", "@overload", ] fail_under = 80 show_missing = true skip_covered = true
[tool.coverage.html] directory = "htmlcov"
[tool.coverage.xml] output = "coverage.xml"

example.title: Django-specific additions to pyproject.toml
example.code:
[tool.pytest.ini_options] DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "config.settings.test" addopts = [ "-v", "--tb=short", "--strict-markers", "--reuse-db", ] markers = [ "slow: marks tests as slow", "integration: marks tests as integration tests", ]
[tool.coverage.run] source = ["apps"] branch = true omit = ["*/migrations/*", "*/tests/*", "*/admin.py"]

section.name: project-structure
section.p: Mirror the source tree in tests/ for easy navigation and pytest auto-discovery:

example.title: Recommended test directory layout
example.code: project/ тФЬтФАтФА src/ тФВ тФФтФАтФА myapp/ тФВ тФЬтФАтФА __init__.py тФВ тФЬтФАтФА models.py тФВ тФФтФАтФА services.py тФЬтФАтФА tests/ тФВ тФЬтФАтФА __init__.py тФВ тФЬтФАтФА conftest.py # Shared fixtures тФВ тФЬтФАтФА unit/ тФВ тФВ тФЬтФАтФА __init__.py тФВ тФВ тФЬтФАтФА test_models.py тФВ тФВ тФФтФАтФА test_services.py тФВ тФЬтФАтФА integration/ тФВ тФВ тФЬтФАтФА __init__.py тФВ тФВ тФФтФАтФА test_api.py тФВ тФФтФАтФА fixtures/ тФВ тФЬтФАтФА factories.py # Factory Boy factories тФВ тФФтФАтФА data.py # Static test data тФФтФАтФА pyproject.toml
example.note: src/myapp/services.py тЖТ tests/unit/test_services.py. The mirroring makes it obvious which tests cover which module.

section.name: coverage-and-parallel
section.p: Branch coverage detects untested conditionals that line coverage misses. Parallel execution reduces CI time on multi-core runners.

example.title: Branch coverage: parametrize for all branches

section.name: ci-integration
section.p: GitHub Actions workflow running pytest with PostgreSQL and Redis services, parallel execution, and coverage upload to Codecov:

example.title: GitHub Actions pytest workflow with services and coverage
example.code:
name: Tests
on: push: branches: [main, develop] pull_request: branches: [main, develop]
env: PYTHON_VERSION: "3.12"
jobs: test: runs-on: ubuntu-latest
services: postgres: image: postgres:16 env: POSTGRES_USER: test POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test POSTGRES_DB: test ports: - 5432:5432 options: >- --health-cmd pg_isready --health-interval 10s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 5
redis: image: redis:7 ports: - 6379:6379 options: >- --health-cmd "redis-cli ping" --health-interval 10s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 5
steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Set up Python uses: actions/setup-python@v5 with: python-version: ${{ env.PYTHON_VERSION }}
- name: Cache pip uses: actions/cache@v4 with: path: ~/.cache/pip key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements*.txt') }} restore-keys: | ${{ runner.os }}-pip-
- name: Install dependencies run: | python -m pip install --upgrade pip pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
- name: Run tests env: DATABASE_URL: postgresql://test:test@localhost:5432/test REDIS_URL: redis://localhost:6379/0 run: | pytest -n auto --cov=src --cov-report=xml --junitxml=junit.xml
- name: Upload coverage uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4 with: files: coverage.xml fail_ci_if_error: true
- name: Upload test results uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 if: always() with: name: test-results path: junit.xml

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Bootstrap sequence for an agent wiring pytest into a new project or migrating from unittest:

how-to.step: Install core plugins: uv add --dev pytest pytest-cov pytest-asyncio pytest-mock pytest-randomly pytest-xdist (add pytest-django and pytest-factoryboy for Django projects).
Configure [tool.pytest.ini_options] in pyproject.toml: testpaths = ["tests"], asyncio_mode = "auto", addopts = ["-ra", "--strict-markers", "--strict-config"].
Configure [tool.coverage.run]: branch = true, parallel = true, source = ["src"], omit migrations and tests. Set fail_under = 80.
Create tests/conftest.py with shared fixtures: database session with rollback, API client (authenticated and unauthenticated), factory fixtures for domain models.
Per module: create tests/test_modulename.py mirroring the source layout. Write the smallest failing test first (red), then implement (green), then expand parametrize for edge cases.
CI: run pytest -n auto --cov --cov-report=xml --cov-fail-under=80; upload coverage.xml to Codecov; upload junit.xml as artifact.

section.p: Agentic TDD prompt pattern for adding tests to a function:
section.prompt: Write pytest tests for <module>.<function>. Cover: - Happy path with realistic input. - Edge cases: empty, None, max-size, off-by-one. - Error paths: each exception class explicitly raised. - @pytest.mark.parametrize for input matrix; provide ids= for readable names. - Use mocker.patch (pytest-mock) over monkeypatch for object methods; autospec=True. Aim for branch coverage >= 90% on the function. Run: pytest -v tests/test_<module>.py --cov=<module> --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing Show output. Fix any uncovered branches before stopping.

Add pytest-asyncio tests for <async_module>.<coroutine>. Setup: - asyncio_mode = "auto" (already in pyproject); no @pytest.mark.asyncio needed. - Use @pytest_asyncio.fixture for async fixtures. - Mock async dependencies with mocker.AsyncMock. - Test concurrent paths: asyncio.gather error propagation; ExceptionGroup handling. - Timeout assertions: with pytest.raises(asyncio.TimeoutError): await asyncio.wait_for(...). Run pytest -v --asyncio-mode=auto.

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Key practices that prevent common long-term problems:

list.item: Mirror source tree in tests/ тАФ src/foo/bar.py maps to tests/foo/test_bar.py. Auto-discoverable, easy to navigate.
Smallest scope first: function-scope fixture is the default; only widen to module/session when measured cost demands it.
Parametrize with ids= тАФ pytest.param(..., id="empty-list") makes failures readable without opening the source.
Mock at the boundary: patch the dependency where it is used in the unit under test, not where it is defined.
mocker.patch.object(cls, "method", autospec=True) prevents drift when the real method's signature changes.
pytest-randomly always on тАФ exposes order-dependence early. Pin seed in CI for reproducibility on failure.
Strict markers + strict config тАФ typos fail the run, not silently mark as no-marker.
filterwarnings = ["error"] turns warnings into failures тАФ catches deprecations before upgrades break you.
Snapshot DRF/JSON shapes with syrupy тАФ diff in PR review surfaces unintended response changes.
Hypothesis for invariants (round-trip serializers, idempotent operations) тАФ catches edge cases parametrize misses.
Do not share state via module globals тАФ fixtures or pytest-randomly will expose the dependency.

checklist.check: pytest and essential plugins installed (pytest-cov, pytest-xdist, pytest-asyncio, pytest-mock, pytest-randomly).
pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] configured with testpaths, strict-markers, and asyncio_mode.
Coverage configured with branch = true, fail_under = 80, and omit patterns for migrations.
tests/ directory created mirroring src/ layout.
tests/conftest.py with shared fixtures (database session, API clients, factories).
CI workflow runs pytest -n auto --cov --cov-report=xml and uploads artifacts.
pytest-randomly installed and --randomly-seed set in CI for reproducible failures.
Custom markers documented in pyproject.toml markers list.

section.name: additional-tools
section.p: Extended tool ecosystem beyond the core set:

list.item: syrupy / pytest-snapshot: snapshot testing for JSON response shapes тАФ diffs surface unintended changes in PR review.
hypothesis: property-based testing for invariants (round-trip serializers, idempotent ops) тАФ finds edge cases parametrize misses.
pytest-recording / vcrpy: record and replay HTTP fixtures; commit cassettes with filter_headers to strip secrets.
respx: httpx-specific mock library with declarative route matching.
freezegun / time-machine: deterministic time mocking for tests with datetime.now() or time.time().
testcontainers-python: spin up postgres/redis Docker containers per session тАФ replaces sqlite-for-tests with realistic databases.
pytest-benchmark: microbenchmark regression detection within the test suite.
pytest-watch / ptw: re-run tests on file changes during development.
Codecov / Coveralls: SaaS coverage tracking with PR comments.
Allure / allure-pytest: rich stakeholder-facing test reports.
Datadog CI Visibility: per-test telemetry for flaky test tracking.
